title: crisp hangover-curing vietnamese colatura bun rieu
description: a fresh, velvety, hangover-curing vietnamese dish featuring colatura, char-grill-yang, and a crispy rice noodle bowl. this recipe is sugar-free friendly.

ingredients.name: colatura
ingredients.notes: use a flavorful, high-quality colatura for the best results

ingredients.name: grilled pork
ingredients.notes: marinate the pork in a mixture of fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar-free sweetner before grilling

ingredients.name: char-grill-yang
ingredients.notes: toast the char-grill-yang seeds to bring out their nutty flavor

ingredients.name: fried shallots
ingredients.notes: use a thermometer to ensure the oil reaches a safe temperature of 160c

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality, sugar-free fish sauce for the best results

ingredients.name: lime juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeeze the lime juice for the best flavor

instructions: cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
heat the colatura in a small saucepan over medium heat.
grill the pork until it reaches an internal temperature of 63c.
toast the char-grill-yang seeds in a small dry pan over medium heat until fragrant.
fry the shallots in a thermometer-equipped frying pan until crispy at 160c.
mix the fish sauce, lime juice, and sugar-free sweetener in a small bowl.
assemle the bun rieu by placing a portion of the cooked noodles in a bowl, topping with the grilled pork, fried shallots, and toasted char-grill-yang seeds, and drizzling with the colatura mixture.
